page: Amazon Web Services m7i.large is a general-purpose instance powered by custom Intel Xeon 8488C processors. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance provides 2 vCPUs derived from a single physical core with hyper-threading enabled. It features a dedicated CPU allocation running at 3.2 GHz, supported by 8.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz. The CPU cache configuration includes 48 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 105 MB of L3 cache. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate moderate single-core performance alongside weaker multi-core and memory capabilities. The instance achieves average ratings in single-core stress-ng and Geekbench tests, as well as single-threaded gzip and bzip3 operations. However, due to its 2-vCPU configuration, multi-core performance is limited, scoring in the bottom 10% for multi-core stress-ng and the bottom 25% for multi-core Geekbench. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, with large-block read and write tests falling into the bottom 10% and 25% respectively. Database operations, Redis SET performance, and static web serving throughput also align with these lower-tier performance brackets.

The m7i.large offers a modern DDR5 memory subsystem and advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512, which assist with specialized vector and matrix operations. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, it serves as an entry-point general-purpose instance for workloads that require dedicated CPU resources but do not demand high multi-core throughput or massive memory bandwidth. Given the lack of local storage and modest network baseline, it is best suited for lightweight general-purpose workloads, basic web serving, small-scale application development, and single-threaded processing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services m7i.large is a general-purpose instance featuring 2 vCPUs and 8.0 GB of DDR5 memory. Powered by a 3.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8488C processor on the x86_64 architecture, it utilizes the AWS Nitro hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show average single-core CPU execution and single-threaded decompression speeds, but multi-core performance and memory bandwidth are limited, placing in the lower percentiles. Supported by advanced instruction sets like Intel AMX and AVX-512, the m7i.large represents an entry-level general-purpose tier. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring dedicated CPU allocation without high parallel processing demands. This instance is best suited for lightweight web serving, development environments, and basic single-threaded applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7i.large is a general-purpose instance featuring 2 vCPUs, 8.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 3.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor, designed for lightweight workloads and development environments.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m7i.large is a general-purpose instance with 2 vCPUs, 8.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 3.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A general-purpose virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processing and DDR5 memory for entry-level workloads and single-threaded applications.
bullet_points: Features 2 vCPUs and 8.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory
Powered by a dedicated 3.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8488C processor
Includes advanced instruction sets such as Intel AMX and AVX-512
Delivers average single-core performance with limited multi-core scaling
Configured with a 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth and no local storage
Best suited for lightweight web serving, development, and single-threaded tasks
